projects.title: AI-Powered Adaptive Feedback in Higher Education
projects.initialIdea: Design an intelligent tutoring agent that reads students' drafts and gives real-time pedagogical feedback based on Bloom's Taxonomy.

projects.title: Socio-Economic Barriers to Renewable Energy Adoption in Rural West Africa
projects.initialIdea: Analyzing why smallholder communities reject solar mini-grids despite high subsidies.

topics.title: Large Language Models as Diagnostic Academic Assessors
topics.description: Evaluating the alignment of generative AI diagnostics against senior human evaluator scores in undergraduate CS coursework.
topics.feedback: High feasibility due to open-source LLM availability. Ensure strict anonymity of training coursework to adhere to institutional guidelines.

topics.title: Visual Cognitive Maps for Prompt Engineering
topics.description: Developing a node-based UI that translates cognitive scaffolds into visual prompt graphs to guide thesis writing.
topics.feedback: Highly original but frontend development overhead may be high for a Master thesis. Scope should focus on a lightweight prototype.

topics.title: Trust, Tariffs, and Tenancy: Landlord-Tenant Dynamics in Mini-Grid Integration
topics.description: An empirical investigation of the landlord-tenant dilemma regarding long-term micro-utility investments in peri-urban markets.
topics.feedback: Excellent focus. PhD rigor requires a strong game-theoretic framework paired with multi-regional survey data.

questions.question: To what extent do LLM-generated pedagogical feedbacks improve subsequent drafting quality in student coursework?
questions.rationale: Directly measures the practical utility and educational impact of the proposed system.
questions.hypothesis: Students utilizing LLM cognitive feedback show a 15% higher score improvement in logic and synthesis during the final draft than control groups.

questions.question: How does the alignment of AI-generated feedback with Bloom's Taxonomy vary across different disciplines?
questions.rationale: Ensures the system generalizes beyond computer science into other scientific fields.

steps.title: Literature Review on Intelligent Tutoring Systems
steps.description: Synthesize findings regarding cognitive tutoring architectures and feedback mechanisms.

steps.title: Define Feedback Taxonomy & Scaffolds
steps.description: Develop the prompt rules mapping student draft metrics to specific Bloom's taxonomy categories.

steps.title: Prototype Writing Evaluation Route
steps.description: Implement API integration with LLM backend for structural text parsing.

steps.title: Human-in-the-Loop Validation Survey
steps.description: Obtain validation scores from university professors reviewing the alignment of generated feedback.

steps.title: Statistical Quality Audit
steps.description: Run ANOVA and descriptive statistical reviews on student draft scores before and after AI scaffold usage.
